revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御霊神社 (大阪市)]][[zh:御靈神社 (大阪市)]]
{{Infobox religious building |name = {{ill|Goryo Shrine|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Shrine|qq=}} |File = [[File:Goryo-Jinja-20071208.jpg|250px]] |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|qid=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}} {{ill|Awajimachi, Osaka|ja|淡路町 (大阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awajimachi, Osaka|1=Awajimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60996461}}4丁目4-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|14.7|N|135|29|57|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q495568}}、{{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|lt=Tsuburahiko-no-Kami|lt_ja=津布良彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|lt=Tsuburahime-no-Kami|lt_ja=津布良媛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}、{{ill|Minamoto no Masanori (Genseirei)|ja|鎌倉権五郎景政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Masanori (Genseirei)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉景正|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1154948}} |type = 旧{{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |festival = 10月16日・17日（例大祭） |神事 = |image = File:Goryo-jinja(Osaka)2.jpg |location = {{ill|Awajimachi|qid=Q60996461|1=Awajimachi}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.68741667|N|135.49916667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''{{ill|Goryo Shrine|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Shrine|qq=}}'''' (ごりょうじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|qid=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}} in the district of {{ill|Awajimachi, Osaka|ja|淡路町 (大阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awajimachi, Osaka|1=Awajimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60996461}}. Its former Ranking was that of an old {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. (Note: The horizontal scroll on its main torii bears the inscription “御霊宮.” Despite its name, this shrine is not connected with {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=Goryo Faith|1=Goryō}}.)
== History == Beginning in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|qid=Q1373157|lt=Kashō|1=Kashō}} (【850】), the site was used as the festival ground for the 八十嶋祭 – held the year after the {{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|qid=Q11433653|lt=Dainōsai|1=Daijosai}} – at a small shrine called 圓神祠 (つぶらしんし) located on the shore of {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|qid=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay|1=Osaka Bay}} at 圓江 (つぶらえ, now {{ill|Tsuubura|ja|靱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuubura|1=Utsubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11663847}}). At that time, the deities enshrined were {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|lt=Tsuburahiko-no-Kami|lt_ja=津布良彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|lt=Tsuburahime-no-Kami|lt_ja=津布良媛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}. Over time the shrine came to be called 圓神社, 圓江神社, or 津村神社 and became the local birthplace shrine (産土神社) for the Tsuwamura area of the former {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}. At that time the shrine was located near {{ill|Kusunaga Shrine|ja|楠永神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunaga Shrine|1=Kusunaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11541236}} in {{ill|Tsuubura-honmachi|ja|靱本町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuubura-honmachi|1=Utsubohonmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60995795}} in the {{ill|Nishi-ku, Osaka|en|Nishi-ku, Osaka|qid=Q275123|lt=Nishi-ku, Osaka|1=Nishi-ku, Osaka}} area.
In the 3rd year of {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|qid=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku|1=Bunroku}} (【1594】), the lord of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and head of the {{ill|Kano-han|ja|鹿野藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano-han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shikano Domain|qid=Q10863418}}, {{ill|Kamei Korenori|en|Kamei Korenori|qid=Q848778|lt=Kamei Tsunakazu|1=Kamei Korenori}}, donated part of his private residence in {{ill|Senba, Osaka|ja|船場 (大阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senba, Osaka|1=Senba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11614009}}. He merged a small shrine dedicated to {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|qid=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} (specifically the one called “乾”) with the enshrined spirit of Minamoto no Masanori (源正霊神) in the main hall and relocated the shrine from 圓江 to its present location in Senba. (Later, Kamei Tsunakazu re-established {{ill|Hakuto Shrine|en|Hakuto Shrine|qid=Q246667|lt=Shirousagi Shrine|1=Hakuto Shrine}} in Inaba Takakusa.)
During the Edo period the shrine also became known by names such as 五郎ノ宮, and in colloquial speech as “ごりょうのみや” or “圓御霊.” In addition, on the southern side of the precincts a temple called 宝城寺 was built with the Eleven-Faced Kannon as its principal image. In the {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} era (1661–1673) the shrine was renamed {{ill|Goryo Shrine|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Shrine|qq=}}, and in the 3rd year of {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|qid=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} (【1753】) it was awarded the Rank of 正一位 by the imperial court.
In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period, the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}} forced the separation of the precincts of Goryo Shrine and 宝城寺; eventually, 宝城寺 became defunct so that today only the torii remains on the south side in front of a company building.
In 【1873】 (Meiji 6) the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gyōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the modern Ranking system. In 【1884】 (Meiji 17) a Bunraku theater called {{ill|Bunraku-za|ja|文楽座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunraku-za|1=Bunraku-za|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500013}} was established on the grounds, operating as “御霊文楽座” and heralding a golden era in the history of Bunraku. However, in November 【1926】 (Taishō 15) a fire broke out in the Bunraku-za, and together with the main hall it was destroyed.
In 【1930】 (Showa 5) the shrine buildings were rebuilt, but on March 13–14, 【1945】 (Showa 20) during the first {{ill|Bombing of Osaka|en|Bombing of Osaka|qid=Q2814395|lt=Osaka Air Raid|1=Bombing of Osaka}}, everything was burned again. In 【1957】 (Showa 32) the current shrine building was reconstructed, and in 【1959】 (Showa 34) the torii with its jeweled fence was rebuilt. In subsequent years the shrine has been enhanced to restore and even surpass its former splendor.
== Deity == * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q495568}} – (associated with {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|lt=Tsuburahiko-no-Kami|lt_ja=津布良彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}} – (the local deity (産土神) of Tsuwamura in the former {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}) * {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|lt=Tsuburahime-no-Kami|lt_ja=津布良媛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}} – (another local deity of Tsuwamura in the former Settsu) * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}} – (also known as Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami) * 源正霊神 – (the spirit (公霊) of {{ill|Minamoto no Masanori (Genseirei)|ja|鎌倉権五郎景政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Masanori (Genseirei)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉景正|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1154948}})
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (本殿) – Rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). * Worship Hall (拝殿) – Rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). * Shrine Office (社務所) * East Palace (東宮) – Encompassing subsidiary shrines: {{ill|Kōdaijingu|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdaijingu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|qid=Q11581011}}, {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine|en|Sarutahiko Shrine|qid=Q11572141|lt=Sarutahiko Shrine|1=Sarutahiko Shrine}}, and the twelve subsidiary shrines of the group ({{ill|Water God Shrine|ja|水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Water God Shrine|1=Suijinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55522725}}, {{ill|Great Thunder Shrine|ja|大雷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Thunder Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Dragon God Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dragon God Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11679331}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja|住吉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉神社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Sugawara Shrine|1=Tenmangū}} (also called 菅原社), Kato Shrine (加藤社), {{ill|Taga Shrine|ja|多賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Shrine|1=Taga shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11431089}} (also called 多賀社), {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}}, {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅神社}} (also known as 事平社), {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q514480}}, Kaminado Shrine (竈戸社), and {{ill|Togakushi Shrine|en|Togakushi Shrine|qid=Q218828|lt=Togakushi Shrine|1=Togakushi Shrine}}). * Matsunoki Shrine (松之木神社) * Daikoku Shrine (大黒社) * Fudō Myōō * Ritual Hall (儀式殿) – Constructed in 1999 (Heisei 11) on the former site of the Goryo {{ill|Bunraku-za|ja|文楽座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunraku-za|1=Bunraku-za|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500013}}; used for weddings, lectures, and other ceremonies. * Monument(s) to the former Goryo Bunraku-za – These stone monuments are not within the shrine grounds proper but are placed at the gate. One such stone pillar inscribed “御霊文楽座跡” was erected by the city, and to the left of the east-facing torii a bronze memorial in the original style was erected by the {{ill|Lions Clubs International|en|Lions Clubs International|qid=Q270029|lt=Lions Club|1=Lions Clubs International}} of {{ill|Kitahama, Osaka|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama, Osaka|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11402355}} on November 3, 1974 (Showa 49). * Offsite Shrine: *** {{ill|Kusunaga Shrine|ja|楠永神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunaga Shrine|1=Kusunaga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11541236}} – Located at 2-1 {{ill|Tsuubura-honmachi|ja|靱本町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuubura-honmachi|1=Utsubohonmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60995795}} in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Osaka|en|Nishi-ku, Osaka|qid=Q275123|lt=Nishi-ku, Osaka|1=Nishi-ku, Osaka}}.
<gallery> 御霊文楽座跡.JPG|Remains of the former Bunraku-za at Goryo Shrine </gallery>
=== Shrine Office (御旅所) === * Horie Gyōmiya (堀江行宮) – Located at 4-28-29 in {{ill|Minamihorie, Osaka|ja|南堀江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihorie, Osaka|1=Minamihorie, Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65249908}} in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Osaka|en|Nishi-ku, Osaka|qid=Q275123|lt=Nishi-ku, Osaka|1=Nishi-ku, Osaka}}. ** Boat processions (船渡御) have been held here since the 9th year of {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1193910}} (【1780】). Although after the Meiji period the processions shifted to land, in 2011 (Heisei 23) the boat procession was revived for the first time in 140 years. <gallery> Goryo-Jinja-Horie-Angu1.jpg|Horie Gyōmiya </gallery>
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * {{Official website}} * {{Webarchive |url=https://archive.fo/AnSu |date=2012nd year 12月11日 |title={{ill|Goryo Shrine (Osaka)|ja|御霊神社 (大阪市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Shrine (Osaka)|1=Goryō Shrine (Osaka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489357}} (Shinto Shrine Exploration & Guardian Dog Chronicles)}} [[ja:御霊神社 (大阪市)]] {{神道 横}}
}}
{{draft categories| <!-- no translated page template found in history --> [[ja:御霊神社 (亀岡市)]] {{translated page|ja|御霊神社 (大阪市)|version=100016490|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11489357}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Former_Fu-sha_in_Osaka]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:ill|Shrines in Osaka City|ja|大阪市の神社}}
